name: apra_regulatory_glossary
description: APRA superannuation reporting standards glossary with regulatory definitions extracted from SRS 101.0 and related standards.

terms.name: Asset Allocation
terms.definition: The process of distributing fund investments across different asset classes, securities, and exposures in accordance with the fund's investment strategy and risk management framework. For APRA reporting purposes, asset allocation must be reported quarterly under SRS 550.0 with particular focus on derivative instrument exposures and counterparty concentrations.

terms.name: Counterparty
terms.definition: A legal entity that enters into a derivative contract or financial arrangement with an RSE Licensee. For APRA reporting, counterparties must be identified using their Legal Entity Identifier (LEI) or interim identifier, and must be reported with all outstanding derivative exposures under SRS 550.0.

terms.name: Derivative
terms.definition: A financial instrument whose value is derived from an underlying asset or reference rate. For APRA purposes, this includes OTC derivatives, exchange-traded derivatives, and cleared derivatives. All derivatives must be reported quarterly under SRS 550.0, including hedges, with counterparty identification and valuation information.

terms.name: Fair Value
terms.definition: The price at which an asset would be exchanged between knowledgeable, willing parties in an arm's length transaction. For APRA reporting under SRS 101.0, fair value is the primary valuation method for all investment assets and derivatives, measured on a mark-to-market basis at quarter end.

terms.name: LEI
terms.definition: A unique 20-character alphanumeric code assigned to legal entities by the Global Legal Entity Identifier Foundation (GLEIF). For APRA reporting under SRS 550.0, LEI is the preferred counterparty identifier format. If LEI is not available, an interim identifier may be used pending LEI registration completion.

terms.name: Margin
terms.definition: Cash or securities held as collateral to secure exchange-traded and cleared derivatives positions, or exchanged with counterparties for OTC derivatives under credit support annexes. Margin arrangements must comply with CPS 226 requirements and be reported under SRS 550.0.

terms.name: Member
terms.definition: An individual who has a superannuation entitlement in a regulated RSE. Members have benefits registered in the fund and are entitled to receive benefit payments in accordance with fund rules and superannuation law. For APRA reporting, member statistics and benefit data are reported under SRS 604.0 and related financial reporting standards.

terms.name: MySuper Product
terms.definition: A standardized superannuation investment product that must be offered by RSE Licensees to members who do not make an active investment choice. MySuper products are subject to specific fee and performance standards under the Superannuation Industry (Supervision) Act 1993 and must be reported under SRS 606.1 and SRS 332.0.

terms.name: RSE Licensee
terms.definition: A company or other legal entity that holds a current license issued by APRA to operate a Registrable Superannuation Entity (RSE). RSE Licensees are responsible for prudent management of fund assets, member benefits, and compliance with APRA reporting standards. All RSE Licensees must submit quarterly and annual returns across the SRS suite of standards.

terms.name: Service Provider
terms.definition: Any external organization that provides operational, technical, or professional services to an RSE Licensee, including custodians, administrators, investment managers, and auditors. RSE Licensees remain accountable for all service provider activities and data submissions, and must ensure service providers comply with data standards and documentation requirements for APRA reporting.

terms.name: Notional Amount
terms.definition: The reference amount used to calculate cash flows for derivative instruments, which may not equal the actual market value of the derivative. For swap contracts, this is typically the principal amount; for options, it is the struck amount. APRA requires both notional and mark-to-market values to be reported in SRS 550.0 for derivatives.

terms.name: Liquidity
terms.definition: The ability to quickly convert investments to cash at fair value without significant loss due to market conditions or asset-specific factors. RSE Licensees must assess and report liquidity profiles of their investment portfolios quarterly under SRS 551.0 to ensure they can meet benefit payment obligations and redemption requests.

terms.name: Repo
terms.definition: A short-term transaction in which securities are sold with an agreement to repurchase them at a specified price and date, effectively acting as a collateralized loan. Repo arrangements, including securities lending and borrowing, must be reported quarterly under SRS 552.0 with details of underlying securities and counterparty information.

terms.name: Gross Exposure
terms.definition: The total absolute value of long and short positions in a portfolio without netting offsetting positions, used to measure the total leverage and concentration of the fund. APRA requires gross exposure reporting under SRS 553.0 to assess the overall leverage and complexity of investment strategies employed by RSE Licensees.

terms.name: Concentration Limit
terms.definition: A maximum permissible exposure to a single counterparty, asset, or asset class, set by an RSE Licensee's investment policy and monitored for compliance. While APRA does not prescribe specific concentration limits, it requires detailed concentration reporting under SRS 550.0 and SRS 553.0 to assess concentration risk and prudential management.
